mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS signals easier discounting and looser financial conditions, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with a falling FEDFUNDS rate, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, the policy rate is restrictive enough to tighten discount rates and credit creation, which should pressure S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the conditional hit rate is not below 45% over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent level marks weakening labor demand and softer household income expectations, which should reduce cyclical equity demand and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the signal's rolling 3-year IC is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth in RSAFS indicates resilient consumer demand and revenue support for large-cap firms, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly RSAFS changes do not correspond to above-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses significance in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST is rising while UNRATE is falling, housing activity and labor income are both improving, which should amplify domestic demand and lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises and UNRATE falls over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered episodes fail to deliver positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects stronger industrial output and better earnings leverage for cyclicals, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly INDPRO changes are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect is absent after accounting for broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: In the months after a GDP slowdown, a rebound in GDPC1 should indicate renewed aggregate demand and improved earnings expectations, which would drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive after a prior quarterly slowdown
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDPC1 rebounds do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the signal is neutral in rolling 4-quarter tests.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS is also rising, inflation pressure forces tighter policy and compresses equity multiples, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises and FEDFUNDS rises in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-plus-hike episodes do not lead to negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the interaction term is insignificant over any 2-year rolling window.
